# 高考作文 — 扫描整合（英文拼写已校对）

> 说明：本文件由文件夹内 19 张图片扫描转写而成，仅做**单词级英文拼写检查**（未改语法）。各篇之间以分隔线区分。全部图片未发现拼写错误。

---

## 01 · 研学旅行提案信

Dear Mr Smith,

Delighted to learn that our school will organize a one-day study trip with the theme of "Chinese Tradition and Modernity" for international students, I am eager to submit my proposal.

First, we will visit a century-old teahouse located in the historic district, where students can experience a traditional Chinese tea ceremony. Following this, we will head to a robotics startup. There, students will see how cutting-edge technology is being integrated into daily life and industry.

The reason for my proposal is that it enables international students to gain a comprehensive understanding of China's development. More importantly, it promotes mutual understanding between cultures. I sincerely hope you will take my proposal into consideration.

Yours,
Li Hua

---

## 02 · China Hi-Tech Exhibition

China Hi-Tech Exhibition

Last weekend, I visited the China Hi-Tech Exhibition with excitement and curiosity.

The exhibition hall showcased cutting-edge inventions, from robots doing tough tasks to new energy vehicles running silently. Scientists showed how AI assisted doctors in surgery, and VR headsets allowed me to "walk" on the moon. Children laughed while controlling drones with simple gestures, demonstrating how technology became accessible even to the youngest users.

I feel proud of our country's scientific progress. These innovations not only help solve real-life problems but also make our daily life easier and greener. The experience inspired me to study harder. I believe technology will shape a brighter future, and we, as the younger generation, must prepare ourselves to contribute.

---

## 03 · Human-AI Interaction 演讲

Good morning, everyone! Recently having participated in a "Human-AI Interaction" program, I was deeply impressed by how quickly AI answered my questions and offered ideas that provided clear explanations and suggested learning resources. The interaction felt natural and helpful, yet I noticed that AI sometimes made mistakes.

To make the most of AI, here are my simple tips. Firstly, ask clear questions since specific prompts generate better answers. Secondly, think critically because AI isn't perfect, and we should check its suggestions carefully. Finally, use it as a tool rather than a replacement for real-world practice.

Powerful as AI is, our creativity and effort matter more, so let's embrace it wisely.

---

## 04 · 海报设计比赛分享信

Dear Peter,

How are you doing these days? I'm writing to share my experience of the poster design competition held by our school last month, whose theme was "Technology Changes Life". I designed a poster about renewable energy, showing how solar and wind power bring convenience to our daily lives.

The whole process went smoothly and efficiently. Having collected a lot of resources online and consulted my science teacher, I finished the poster in three days. I used a before-and-after comparison to highlight the advantages of renewable energy, which made the poster more convincing.

This experience was extremely rewarding. It was not until I participated in the competition that I truly understood the charm of combining book knowledge with practice. And I also made up my mind to dive deeper into science and technology.

---

## 05 · Campus Labor Week 通知

Dear fellow students,

To promote the spirit of labor and cultivate practical skills, our school will launch the Campus Labor Week from May 10th to 16th.

This event is characterized by a variety of meaningful activities. Participants can choose to join the organic farming program at the school garden, learning planting techniques under teachers' guidance. Alternatively, the handicraft workshop offers opportunities to create traditional clay sculptures using eco-friendly materials. For those who are interested in social services, community cleanup campaigns will be organized near the campus.

We sincerely appeal for your active participation to jointly make this event a success.

Student Union

---

## 06 · When Chinese Poetry Meets English Poetry

When Chinese Poetry Meets English Poetry

Last week, our school's Teaching Innovation Week featured a unique poetry appreciation class co-taught by teachers of Chinese and English.

The innovative lesson focused on comparing Chinese classical poems with renowned English classical poems. By utilizing multimedia tools, the teachers highlighted the rhythmic beauty of poetry from both traditions. Students actively participated in discussions, exploring cultural differences and similarities. This cross-cultural educational experience deepened our understanding of Chinese and English poetry. It also showed the power of interdisciplinary learning in practice.

As far as I'm concerned, the class broadened my perspective on how different cultures express universal human emotions. It successfully created a bridge across cultural divides.

---

## 07 · Happy Farm 介绍

Hello, everyone! I'm Li Hua, in charge of our school's newly-built Happy Farm. It's my honor to give a brief introduction to you. We set up the farm with several meaningful goals, all aimed at the better development of us students. We hope to help students cultivate a sense of responsibility, enhance teamwork skills, and appreciate the value of hard work.

Here at Happy Farm, we have designed a variety of activities, through which we can learn essential farming procedures: plowing the field, sowing seeds, tending to vegetables and harvesting produce. Meanwhile, we'll also host lectures on smart agriculture to broaden our understanding of modern farming technologies.

That's all about our farm. Feel free to ask any questions. Thank you!

---

## 08 · 共读邀请信

Dear David,

How's everything going? I'm writing to invite you to participate in our school's upcoming online exchange event, "Read a Book with Overseas Pen Friends", which will be held next month. The activity aims to help us learn from each other through shared reading experiences.

Here is how it works: We'll select a book together, and then meet online every two weeks to share our thoughts. I am wondering if you would be interested in reading The Analects. It features Confucius' wisdom and offers an accessible introduction to Chinese cultural values. It's a bilingual edition, so the language shouldn't be too challenging. Of course, if you have other suggestions, I'd love to hear them. I really hope you'll be able to join us! If this appeals to you, please e-mail me back by this weekend.

Looking forward to hearing from you!

Yours,
Li Hua

---

## 09 · Will Paper Books Disappear?

Will Paper Books Disappear?

Good morning, everyone! As we gather here on World Book Day, I'm honored to speak about whether paper books will disappear.

From my perspective, physical books cannot be replaced, despite the rise of digital reading. First, physical books offer a unique experience that e-books can't match, which helps readers become more focused. Moreover, the sensory experience—turning pages, feeling the texture, smelling the ink—creates an emotional connection that screens cannot give us. What can't be ignored is that excessive digital reading can even bring physical and psychological harm, especially to the young. These factors make physical books invaluable to readers and society alike.

In short, paper books are more than reading tools; they are cultural treasures. Technology may change, but our love for paper books will endure. Thank you for listening!

---

## 10 · Sports Make Me Better

Sports Make Me Better

Among various sports, I've been sticking to running. Every weekday morning, I run around the school playground for half an hour. On weekends, I usually run in a nearby park for about 40 minutes.

Running has brought me two significant changes. First, my physical health has greatly improved—I rarely catch a cold now. Second, running has made me more disciplined and confident, as keeping a routine requires self-control and brings a sense of achievement.

Let's make good use of our spare time and take an active part in sports. It not only benefits our health but also helps make us better.

---

## 11 · 活动改至室内通知

Notice

We regret to inform you that the outdoor sports and cultural exchange activity, initially set for this Friday, has to be moved indoors to the school gym. This is because the weather forecast has issued a warning of heavy rain and strong winds.

Please note the following: Firstly, arrive at the gym 20 minutes before the scheduled start time to ensure a proper warm-up. Secondly, wear comfortable sportswear and proper sports shoes to ensure flexibility and safety during the activities. Thirdly, respect the equipment in the gym and follow the instructions of the staff.

We are confident this will still be an exciting and engaging event. Looking forward to seeing you all there!

International Culture Exchange Club

---

## 12 · 环保参观建议信

Dear James,

I'm thrilled to hear about your plan to visit China, especially given your interest in our environmental achievements. Here are my suggestions for your visit.

You might want to consider the Yangtze River region, where major conservation projects have helped restore water quality and biodiversity after years of pollution. It's a powerful example of ecological recovery alongside economic growth. Another great option could be Saihanba—once a desert, now a vast forest known as the "Great Green Wall". It shows how sustained afforestation can combat desertification.

I believe this trip will help you gain a deeper understanding of China's efforts. Looking forward to your arrival.

Yours,
Li Hua

---

## 13 · Green Living Initiative

Dear Jim,

Delighted to hear that you are interested in the Green Living Initiative in our city! I'd love to share something about it. This city-wide campaign was launched last month to raise public awareness about environmental protection and promote low-carbon lifestyles.

In response to the call of the campaign, I took part in the Community Cycling Event. I, together with hundreds of residents, cycled along a designated route, waving flags to advocate riding bikes. Though tired, I felt proud to be part of this collective effort, which would make a difference to our living environment.

I believe this initiative will help contribute to a more sustainable city. Do you have any green activities like this in your city? I'm curious to know!

Yours,
Li Hua

---

## 14 · 合作学习反馈信

Dear Jason,

I'm writing to share my feedback on cooperative learning applied in our English classes this term. Cooperative learning has become an essential part of our learning process. We've had group discussions, team projects, and various collaborative activities, in which we have enjoyed a refreshing change from traditional teaching methods.

From my perspective, the experience has been quite positive. I find that working in groups not only enhances our understanding of the material but also improves our communication and teamwork skills. It's an extremely engaging way to learn and has boosted my confidence in using English.

Had it not been for this method, I wouldn't have grown so much. Thank you for introducing it!

Yours,
Li Hua

---

## 15 · 学科融合建议信

Dear Janet,

I'm glad to hear that you are working to enrich your English classes by integrating content from other subjects. I am truly excited about this creative initiative and would like to propose combining history and art into your English lessons.

In history-themed sessions, we can discuss ancient Chinese civilization in English, thereby learning vocabulary and gaining a deeper understanding of how to use these words in context. Similarly, in art-themed sessions, describing famous paintings or talking about color theory in English helps us practice descriptive language naturally.

Such methods would make English learning more vivid and meaningful, because we can use English to explore topics we are interested in rather than memorizing vocabulary in isolation. Undoubtedly, this teaching method will greatly boost our enthusiasm for learning English!

---

## 16 · 射箭看图作文（坚持）

In the first picture, a young man tries repeatedly to shoot arrows at a target but fails to hit the center. He looks frustrated and wonders whether all his efforts have been in vain. The second picture tells a sadder story: The man gives up and throws his bow and arrows into a trash can, leaving the target behind.

From my perspective, success isn't about never missing the target, but about keeping the bow in our hand. What truly matters is perseverance, for it is through persistent efforts that we learn and improve. If we give up too early, we may miss the very moment when success is within reach. Only by continuing can we ensure that our efforts eventually bear fruit.

So don't let the bow and arrows end up in the trash can. The target hasn't moved; only our persistence can bring it within reach.

---

## 17 · 理性使用 AI 演讲

Fellow students,

Recently, I've noticed a growing number of our classmates turning to AI assistants for academic tasks. While AI tools undoubtedly boost efficiency and offer instant solutions, I'm here to remind us that over-reliance on AI risks undermining our independent learning capabilities.

So, how should we move forward? I suggest using AI as a consultant rather than a substitute for our own thinking. For example, when facing challenging problems, we can first try to figure them out by ourselves, and then use AI to check answers and explore different perspectives. Additionally, we should set aside daily "tech-free" study sessions to strengthen foundational skills and self-discipline.

Let's embrace AI wisely! Let it boost our creativity, not replace our curiosity. After all, the goal of education isn't just finding answers—it's training our minds to ask better questions. Thank you for listening.

---

## 18 · My Views on the New Schedule

My Views on the New Schedule

I'm really excited about the new schedule that our school will introduce next term, which reflects a thoughtful shift toward students' well-being. The extended break between classes allows us to relax and recharge better, and having daily PE classes is a great way to stay fit. Moreover, the longer self-study time will help us better manage our studies.

With this new schedule, I plan to use the extended break time to take short walks outside the classroom, giving my eyes and mind a complete reset. During the PE classes, I'll actively participate in various sports activities to improve my physical condition. As for the extended self-study time, I'll make a detailed plan to review and preview my courses.

In conclusion, I believe the new schedule will help cultivate all-round students. Not only will it benefit our physical health, but it will also help us improve our academic performance.

---

## 19 · 讲座邀请信

Dear Mr Smith,

I'm Li Hua, president of the Student Union. On behalf of the Senior 3 students, I'm writing to invite you to deliver a speech at our upcoming lecture on "Stress Management and Positive Attitude". It will be held in the school hall at 2:30 pm next Friday.

As we are facing heavy study pressure, we need your professional advice to stay positive. We know you have rich experience in helping students deal with stress, which is why we hope you can share practical methods with us. We also want to learn how foreign students manage stress, and this will surely benefit us a lot.

Could you please let me know if you can come?

Looking forward to your reply.

Yours sincerely,
Li Hua
